revision.comment: Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:شوئیچی]][[ja:正一位]][[ko:정1위]][[zh:正一位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:شوئیچی]][[ja:正一位]][[ko:정1위]][[zh:正一位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:شوئیچی]][[ja:正一位]][[ko:정1위]][[zh:正一位]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11123258]] Wikidata name: Senior First Rank [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shōichii''' ({{ill|Senior First Rank|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}) is one of the highest [[Court Ranks in Japan|Ranks]] in Japan's system of [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō]]-based hierarchy and [[Shinkai (divine rank)|Shinkai (divine Ranks)]]. It Ranks above {{ill|Junior First Rank|ja|従一位|1=Junior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview == The Rank of Shōichii has been historically conferred upon individuals who made extraordinary contributions to the state, such as {{ill|Kampaku|ja|関白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kampaku|1=kampaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1199966}}, [[:en:Daijō-daijin|Daijō Daijin]], or [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]. However, only seven individuals have ever received this Rank during their lifetime.<ref group="Note">These include {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}}, [[:en:Fujiwara no Muchimaro|Fujiwara no Muchimaro]], [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagate|Fujiwara no Nagate]], {{ill|Minamoto no Katako|ja|源方子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Katako|1=Minamoto no Masako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564108}}, and [[:en:Sanjō Sanetomi|Sanetomi Sanjō]]. Two of these individuals, Fujiwara no Muchimaro and Sanetomi Sanjō, passed away on the same day they received the Rank.</ref> Most instances of this Rank were posthumously awarded as {{ill|Zoi|lt=Zoi|lt=Zōi|ja|贈位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōi|qq=}}.
After [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Rank was formalized under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|lt=Shokuinryō|lt=Shokuinrei|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinrei|qq=}} of 1869.<ref>{{Egov law|215IO0000000325|位階令}}</ref> However, the Rank became symbolic as the role's corresponding duties ceased to exist.
For [[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|Shin’i]], many shrines achieved the Rank of Shōichii over time, particularly with the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrines]], which adopted this Rank after the main shrine, [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari Taisha]], received imperial recognition.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://inari.jp/about/history/num04/ |title=稲荷勧請 |publisher=伏見稲荷大社 |accessdate=2024-01-21}}</ref>
== Shrines Awarded Shōichii == * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] – 807 AD * [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Shrine]] – 850 AD * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Shrine]] * [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]]
== Notable Individuals Awarded Shōichii == '''Bold''' indicates those awarded during their lifetime.
{| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Date of Conferment !! Notes |- | [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]] || [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 4 (720 AD) || Minister of the Right |- | '''{{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}}''' || [[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinkō]] 1 (724 AD) || Consort of Emperor Monmu |- | [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagate|Fujiwara no Nagate]] || [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] 1 (770 AD) || Minister of the Left |- | [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] || [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 6 (1917 AD) || Daimyō, posthumous award |}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kan'i|ja|官位|1=East Asian government service ranking|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'i|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'i system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452076}} * {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=Shin’i|ja|神位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai (divine rank)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神階}} * {{ill|Zoi|lt=Zoi|lt=Zōi|ja|贈位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōi|qq=}} * {{ill|IRankai System|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/IRankai System|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}}

However, only seven individuals have ever received this Rank during their lifetime.<ref group="Note">These include {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}}, [[:en:Fujiwara no Muchimaro|Fujiwara no Muchimaro]], [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagate|Fujiwara no Nagate]], {{ill|Minamoto no Katako|ja|源方子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Katako|1=Minamoto no Masako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564108}}, and [[:en:Sanjō Sanetomi|Sanetomi Sanjō]]. Two of these individuals, Fujiwara no Muchimaro and Sanetomi Sanjō, passed away on the same day they received the Rank.</ref> Most instances of this Rank were posthumously awarded as {{ill|Zoi|lt=Zoi|lt=Zōi|ja|贈位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōi|qq=}}.
After [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Rank was formalized under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|lt=Shokuinryō|lt=Shokuinrei|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinrei|qq=}} of 1869.<ref>{{Egov law|215IO0000000325|位階令}}</ref> However, the Rank became symbolic as the role's corresponding duties ceased to exist.
For [[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|Shin’i]], many shrines achieved the Rank of Shōichii over time, particularly with the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrines]], which adopted this Rank after the main shrine, [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari Taisha]], received imperial recognition.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://inari.jp/about/history/num04/ |title=稲荷勧請 |publisher=伏見稲荷大社 |accessdate=2024-01-21}}</ref>
== Shrines Awarded Shōichii == * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] – 807 AD * [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Shrine]] – 850 AD * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Shrine]] * [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:شوئیچی]][[ja:正一位]][[ko:정1위]][[zh:正一位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:شوئیچی]][[ja:正一位]][[ko:정1위]][[zh:正一位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:شوئیچی]][[ja:正一位]][[ko:정1위]][[zh:正一位]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11123258]] Wikidata name: Senior First Rank [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shōichii''' ({{ill|Senior First Rank|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}) is one of the highest [[Court Ranks in Japan|Ranks]] in Japan's system of [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō]]-based hierarchy and [[Shinkai (divine rank)|Shinkai (divine Ranks)]]. It Ranks above {{ill|Junior First Rank|ja|従一位|1=Junior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview == The Rank of Shōichii has been historically conferred upon individuals who made extraordinary contributions to the state, such as {{ill|Kampaku|ja|関白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kampaku|1=kampaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1199966}}, [[:en:Daijō-daijin|Daijō Daijin]], or [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]. However, only seven individuals have ever received this Rank during their lifetime.<ref group="Note">These include {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}}, [[:en:Fujiwara no Muchimaro|Fujiwara no Muchimaro]], [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagate|Fujiwara no Nagate]], {{ill|Minamoto no Katako|ja|源方子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Katako|1=Minamoto no Masako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564108}}, and [[:en:Sanjō Sanetomi|Sanetomi Sanjō]]. Two of these individuals, Fujiwara no Muchimaro and Sanetomi Sanjō, passed away on the same day they received the Rank.</ref> Most instances of this Rank were posthumously awarded as {{ill|Zoi|lt=Zoi|lt=Zōi|ja|贈位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōi|qq=}}.
After [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Rank was formalized under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|lt=Shokuinryō|lt=Shokuinrei|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinrei|qq=}} of 1869.<ref>{{Egov law|215IO0000000325|位階令}}</ref> However, the Rank became symbolic as the role's corresponding duties ceased to exist.
For [[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|Shin’i]], many shrines achieved the Rank of Shōichii over time, particularly with the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrines]], which adopted this Rank after the main shrine, [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari Taisha]], received imperial recognition.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://inari.jp/about/history/num04/ |title=稲荷勧請 |publisher=伏見稲荷大社 |accessdate=2024-01-21}}</ref>
== Shrines Awarded Shōichii == * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] – 807 AD * [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Shrine]] – 850 AD * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Shrine]] * [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]]
== Notable Individuals Awarded Shōichii == '''Bold''' indicates those awarded during their lifetime.
{| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Date of Conferment !! Notes |- | [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]] || [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 4 (720 AD) || Minister of the Right |- | '''{{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}}''' || [[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinkō]] 1 (724 AD) || Consort of Emperor Monmu |- | [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagate|Fujiwara no Nagate]] || [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] 1 (770 AD) || Minister of the Left |- | [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] || [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 6 (1917 AD) || Daimyō, posthumous award |}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kan'i|ja|官位|1=East Asian government service ranking|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'i|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'i system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452076}} * {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=Shin’i|ja|神位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai (divine rank)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神階}} * {{ill|Zoi|lt=Zoi|lt=Zōi|ja|贈位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōi|qq=}} * {{ill|IRankai System|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/IRankai System|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}}
== References == <references group="Note" /> {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|E-Gov Law Search|ja|E-Gov法令検索|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E-Gov Law Search|qq=}} * [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Collection

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:شوئیچی]][[ja:正一位]][[ko:정1위]][[zh:正一位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:شوئیچی]][[ja:正一位]][[ko:정1위]][[zh:正一位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:شوئیچی]][[ja:正一位]][[ko:정1위]][[zh:正一位]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11123258]] Wikidata name: Senior First Rank [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shōichii''' ({{ill|Senior First Rank|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}) is one of the highest [[Court Ranks in Japan|Ranks]] in Japan's system of [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō]]-based hierarchy and [[Shinkai (divine rank)|Shinkai (divine Ranks)]]. It Ranks above {{ill|Junior First Rank|ja|従一位|1=Junior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview == The Rank of Shōichii has been historically conferred upon individuals who made extraordinary contributions to the state, such as {{ill|Kampaku|ja|関白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kampaku|1=kampaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1199966}}, [[:en:Daijō-daijin|Daijō Daijin]], or [[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]. However, only seven individuals have ever received this Rank during their lifetime.<ref group="Note">These include {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}}, [[:en:Fujiwara no Muchimaro|Fujiwara no Muchimaro]], [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]], [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagate|Fujiwara no Nagate]], {{ill|Minamoto no Katako|ja|源方子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Katako|1=Minamoto no Masako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564108}}, and [[:en:Sanjō Sanetomi|Sanetomi Sanjō]]. Two of these individuals, Fujiwara no Muchimaro and Sanetomi Sanjō, passed away on the same day they received the Rank.</ref> Most instances of this Rank were posthumously awarded as {{ill|Zoi|lt=Zoi|lt=Zōi|ja|贈位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōi|qq=}}.
After [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], the Rank was formalized under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|lt=Shokuinryō|lt=Shokuinrei|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinrei|qq=}} of 1869.<ref>{{Egov law|215IO0000000325|位階令}}</ref> However, the Rank became symbolic as the role's corresponding duties ceased to exist.
For [[:en:Shinkai (divine rank)|Shin’i]], many shrines achieved the Rank of Shōichii over time, particularly with the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrines]], which adopted this Rank after the main shrine, [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari Taisha]], received imperial recognition.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://inari.jp/about/history/num04/ |title=稲荷勧請 |publisher=伏見稲荷大社 |accessdate=2024-01-21}}</ref>
== Shrines Awarded Shōichii == * [[:en:Kamo shrines|Kamo Shrine]] – 807 AD * [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Shrine]] – 850 AD * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|qq=}} * [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Shrine]] * [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]]
== Notable Individuals Awarded Shōichii == '''Bold''' indicates those awarded during their lifetime.
{| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Date of Conferment !! Notes |- | [[:en:Fujiwara no Fuhito|Fujiwara no Fuhito]] || [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 4 (720 AD) || Minister of the Right |- | '''{{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}}''' || [[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinkō]] 1 (724 AD) || Consort of Emperor Monmu |- | [[:en:Fujiwara no Nagate|Fujiwara no Nagate]] || [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] 1 (770 AD) || Minister of the Left |- | [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] || [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 6 (1917 AD) || Daimyō, posthumous award |}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kan'i|ja|官位|1=East Asian government service ranking|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'i|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'i system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452076}} * {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=Shin’i|ja|神位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai (divine rank)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神階}} * {{ill|Zoi|lt=Zoi|lt=Zōi|ja|贈位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōi|qq=}} * {{ill|IRankai System|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/IRankai System|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}}

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:شوئیچی]][[ja:正一位]][[ko:정1위]][[zh:正一位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:شوئیچی]][[ja:正一位]][[ko:정1위]][[zh:正一位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:شوئیچی]][[ja:正一位]][[ko:정1위]][[zh:正一位]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11123258]] Wikidata name: Senior First Rank [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shōichii''' ({{ill|Senior First Rank|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}) is one of the highest [[Court Ranks in Japan|Ranks]] in Japan's system of {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō}}-based hierarchy and [[Shinkai (divine rank)|Shinkai (divine Ranks)]]. It Ranks above {{ill|Junior First Rank|ja|従一位|1=Junior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview == The Rank of Shōichii has been historically conferred upon individuals who made extraordinary contributions to the state, such as {{ill|Kampaku|ja|関白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kampaku|1=kampaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1199966}}, {{ill|Daijō-daijin|en|Daijō-daijin|WD=Q1157586|lt=Daijō Daijin}}, or {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}}. However, only seven individuals have ever received this Rank during their lifetime.<ref group="Note">These include {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|en|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|WD=Q1890878|lt=Fujiwara no Muchimaro}}, {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|en|Tachibana no Moroe|WD=Q3135863|lt=Tachibana no Moroe}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagate|en|Fujiwara no Nagate|WD=Q11623368|lt=Fujiwara no Nagate}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Katako|ja|源方子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Katako|1=Minamoto no Masako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564108}}, and {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|en|Sanjō Sanetomi|WD=Q516646|lt=Sanetomi Sanjō}}. Two of these individuals, Fujiwara no Muchimaro and Sanetomi Sanjō, passed away on the same day they received the Rank.</ref> Most instances of this Rank were posthumously awarded as {{ill|Zoi|lt=Zoi|lt=Zōi|ja|贈位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōi|qq=}}.
After {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, the Rank was formalized under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|lt=Shokuinryō|lt=Shokuinrei|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinrei|qq=}} of 1869.<ref>{{Egov law|215IO0000000325|位階令}}</ref> However, the Rank became symbolic as the role's corresponding duties ceased to exist.
For {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|en|Shinkai (divine rank)|WD=Q11591025|lt=Shin’i}}, many shrines achieved the Rank of Shōichii over time, particularly with the {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrines}}, which adopted this Rank after the main shrine, {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|en|Fushimi Inari-taisha|WD=Q714828|lt=Fushimi Inari Taisha}}, received imperial recognition.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://inari.jp/about/history/num04/ |title=稲荷勧請 |publisher=伏見稲荷大社 |accessdate=2024-01-21}}</ref>
== Shrines Awarded Shōichii == * {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrine}} – 807 AD * {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Shrine}} – 850 AD * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Shrine}} * {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha}}
== Notable Individuals Awarded Shōichii == '''Bold''' indicates those awarded during their lifetime.
{| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Date of Conferment !! Notes |- | {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|WD=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} || {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} 4 (720 AD) || Minister of the Right |- | '''{{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}}''' || {{ill|Jinki (era)|en|Jinki (era)|WD=Q1373210|lt=Shinkō}} 1 (724 AD) || Consort of Emperor Monmu |- | {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagate|en|Fujiwara no Nagate|WD=Q11623368|lt=Fujiwara no Nagate}} || {{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|WD=Q18520676|lt=Hōki}} 1 (770 AD) || Minister of the Left |- | {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga}} || {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō}} 6 (1917 AD) || Daimyō, posthumous award |}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kan'i|ja|官位|1=East Asian government service ranking|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'i|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'i system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452076}} * {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=Shin’i|ja|神位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai (divine rank)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神階}} * {{ill|Zoi|lt=Zoi|lt=Zōi|ja|贈位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōi|qq=}} * {{ill|IRankai System|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/IRankai System|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}}
== References == <references group="Note" /> {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=215IO0000000325 位階令] - {{ill|E-Gov Law Search|ja|E-Gov法令検索|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/E-Gov Law Search|qq=}} * [{{NDLDC|787949/168}} 官位相当表] - National Diet Library Digital Collection
{{Court Ranks of Japan}} *

However, only seven individuals have ever received this Rank during their lifetime.<ref group="Note">These include {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|en|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|WD=Q1890878|lt=Fujiwara no Muchimaro}}, {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|en|Tachibana no Moroe|WD=Q3135863|lt=Tachibana no Moroe}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagate|en|Fujiwara no Nagate|WD=Q11623368|lt=Fujiwara no Nagate}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Katako|ja|源方子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Katako|1=Minamoto no Masako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564108}}, and {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|en|Sanjō Sanetomi|WD=Q516646|lt=Sanetomi Sanjō}}. Two of these individuals, Fujiwara no Muchimaro and Sanetomi Sanjō, passed away on the same day they received the Rank.</ref> Most instances of this Rank were posthumously awarded as {{ill|Zoi|lt=Zoi|lt=Zōi|ja|贈位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōi|qq=}}.
After {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, the Rank was formalized under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|lt=Shokuinryō|lt=Shokuinrei|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinrei|qq=}} of 1869.<ref>{{Egov law|215IO0000000325|位階令}}</ref> However, the Rank became symbolic as the role's corresponding duties ceased to exist.

== Shrines Awarded Shōichii == * {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrine}} – 807 AD * {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Shrine}} – 850 AD * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Shrine}} * {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha}}

{| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Date of Conferment !! Notes |- | {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|WD=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} || {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} 4 (720 AD) || Minister of the Right |- | '''{{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}}''' || {{ill|Jinki (era)|en|Jinki (era)|WD=Q1373210|lt=Shinkō}} 1 (724 AD) || Consort of Emperor Monmu |- | {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagate|en|Fujiwara no Nagate|WD=Q11623368|lt=Fujiwara no Nagate}} || {{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|WD=Q18520676|lt=Hōki}} 1 (770 AD) || Minister of the Left |- | {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga}} || {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō}} 6 (1917 AD) || Daimyō, posthumous award |}

It Ranks above {{ill|Junior First Rank|ja|従一位|1=Junior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview == The Rank of Shōichii has been historically conferred upon individuals who made extraordinary contributions to the state, such as {{ill|Kampaku|ja|関白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kampaku|1=kampaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1199966}}, {{ill|Daijō-daijin|en|Daijō-daijin|WD=Q1157586|lt=Daijō Daijin}}, or {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}}. However, only seven individuals have ever received this Rank during their lifetime.<ref group="Note">These include {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|en|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|WD=Q1890878|lt=Fujiwara no Muchimaro}}, {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|en|Tachibana no Moroe|WD=Q3135863|lt=Tachibana no Moroe}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagate|en|Fujiwara no Nagate|WD=Q11623368|lt=Fujiwara no Nagate}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Katako|ja|源方子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Katako|1=Minamoto no Masako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564108}}, and {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|en|Sanjō Sanetomi|WD=Q516646|lt=Sanetomi Sanjō}}. Two of these individuals, Fujiwara no Muchimaro and Sanetomi Sanjō, passed away on the same day they received the Rank.</ref> Most instances of this Rank were posthumously awarded as {{ill|Zoi|lt=Zoi|lt=Zōi|ja|贈位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōi|qq=}}.
After {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, the Rank was formalized under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|lt=Shokuinryō|lt=Shokuinrei|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinrei|qq=}} of 1869.<ref>{{Egov law|215IO0000000325|位階令}}</ref> However, the Rank became symbolic as the role's corresponding duties ceased to exist.
For {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|en|Shinkai (divine rank)|WD=Q11591025|lt=Shin’i}}, many shrines achieved the Rank of Shōichii over time, particularly with the {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrines}}, which adopted this Rank after the main shrine, {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|en|Fushimi Inari-taisha|WD=Q714828|lt=Fushimi Inari Taisha}}, received imperial recognition.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://inari.jp/about/history/num04/ |title=稲荷勧請 |publisher=伏見稲荷大社 |accessdate=2024-01-21}}</ref>
== Shrines Awarded Shōichii == * {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrine}} – 807 AD * {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Shrine}} – 850 AD * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Shrine}} * {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha}}
== Notable Individuals Awarded Shōichii == '''Bold''' indicates those awarded during their lifetime.
{| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Date of Conferment !! Notes |- | {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|WD=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} || {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} 4 (720 AD) || Minister of the Right |- | '''{{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}}''' || {{ill|Jinki (era)|en|Jinki (era)|WD=Q1373210|lt=Shinkō}} 1 (724 AD) || Consort of Emperor Monmu |- | {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagate|en|Fujiwara no Nagate|WD=Q11623368|lt=Fujiwara no Nagate}} || {{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|WD=Q18520676|lt=Hōki}} 1 (770 AD) || Minister of the Left |- | {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga}} || {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō}} 6 (1917 AD) || Daimyō, posthumous award |}
== See Also == * {{ill|Kan'i|ja|官位|1=East Asian government service ranking|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'i|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'i system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452076}} * {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|lt=Shin’i|ja|神位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkai (divine rank)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神階}} * {{ill|Zoi|lt=Zoi|lt=Zōi|ja|贈位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōi|qq=}} * {{ill|IRankai System|ja|位階令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/IRankai System|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 位階|1=court rank in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q99196082}}

'''Shōichii''' ({{ill|Senior First Rank|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}) is one of the highest [[Court Ranks in Japan|Ranks]] in Japan's system of {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō}}-based hierarchy and [[Shinkai (divine rank)|Shinkai (divine Ranks)]]. It Ranks above {{ill|Junior First Rank|ja|従一位|1=Junior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview == The Rank of Shōichii has been historically conferred upon individuals who made extraordinary contributions to the state, such as {{ill|Kampaku|ja|関白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kampaku|1=kampaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1199966}}, {{ill|Daijō-daijin|en|Daijō-daijin|WD=Q1157586|lt=Daijō Daijin}}, or {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}}. However, only seven individuals have ever received this Rank during their lifetime.<ref group="Note">These include {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|en|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|WD=Q1890878|lt=Fujiwara no Muchimaro}}, {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|en|Tachibana no Moroe|WD=Q3135863|lt=Tachibana no Moroe}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagate|en|Fujiwara no Nagate|WD=Q11623368|lt=Fujiwara no Nagate}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Katako|ja|源方子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Katako|1=Minamoto no Masako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564108}}, and {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|en|Sanjō Sanetomi|WD=Q516646|lt=Sanetomi Sanjō}}. Two of these individuals, Fujiwara no Muchimaro and Sanetomi Sanjō, passed away on the same day they received the Rank.</ref> Most instances of this Rank were posthumously awarded as {{ill|Zoi|lt=Zoi|lt=Zōi|ja|贈位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōi|qq=}}.
After {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, the Rank was formalized under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|lt=Shokuinryō|lt=Shokuinrei|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinrei|qq=}} of 1869.<ref>{{Egov law|215IO0000000325|位階令}}</ref> However, the Rank became symbolic as the role's corresponding duties ceased to exist.
For {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|en|Shinkai (divine rank)|WD=Q11591025|lt=Shin’i}}, many shrines achieved the Rank of Shōichii over time, particularly with the {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrines}}, which adopted this Rank after the main shrine, {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|en|Fushimi Inari-taisha|WD=Q714828|lt=Fushimi Inari Taisha}}, received imperial recognition.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://inari.jp/about/history/num04/ |title=稲荷勧請 |publisher=伏見稲荷大社 |accessdate=2024-01-21}}</ref>
== Shrines Awarded Shōichii == * {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrine}} – 807 AD * {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Shrine}} – 850 AD * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Shrine}} * {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha}}

Notes |- | {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|WD=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} || {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} 4 (720 AD) || Minister of the Right |- | '''{{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}}''' || {{ill|Jinki (era)|en|Jinki (era)|WD=Q1373210|lt=Shinkō}} 1 (724 AD) || Consort of Emperor Monmu |- | {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagate|en|Fujiwara no Nagate|WD=Q11623368|lt=Fujiwara no Nagate}} || {{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|WD=Q18520676|lt=Hōki}} 1 (770 AD) || Minister of the Left |- | {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga}} || {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō}} 6 (1917 AD) || Daimyō, posthumous award |}

It Ranks above {{ill|Junior First Rank|ja|従一位|1=Junior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview == The Rank of Shōichii has been historically conferred upon individuals who made extraordinary contributions to the state, such as {{ill|Kampaku|ja|関白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kampaku|1=kampaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1199966}}, {{ill|Daijō-daijin|en|Daijō-daijin|WD=Q1157586|lt=Daijō Daijin}}, or {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}}. However, only seven individuals have ever received this Rank during their lifetime.<ref group="Note">These include {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|en|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|WD=Q1890878|lt=Fujiwara no Muchimaro}}, {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|en|Tachibana no Moroe|WD=Q3135863|lt=Tachibana no Moroe}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagate|en|Fujiwara no Nagate|WD=Q11623368|lt=Fujiwara no Nagate}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Katako|ja|源方子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Katako|1=Minamoto no Masako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564108}}, and {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|en|Sanjō Sanetomi|WD=Q516646|lt=Sanetomi Sanjō}}. Two of these individuals, Fujiwara no Muchimaro and Sanetomi Sanjō, passed away on the same day they received the Rank.</ref> Most instances of this Rank were posthumously awarded as {{ill|Zoi|lt=Zoi|lt=Zōi|ja|贈位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōi|qq=}}.
After {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, the Rank was formalized under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|lt=Shokuinryō|lt=Shokuinrei|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinrei|qq=}} of 1869.<ref>{{Egov law|215IO0000000325|位階令}}</ref> However, the Rank became symbolic as the role's corresponding duties ceased to exist.
For {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|en|Shinkai (divine rank)|WD=Q11591025|lt=Shin’i}}, many shrines achieved the Rank of Shōichii over time, particularly with the {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrines}}, which adopted this Rank after the main shrine, {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|en|Fushimi Inari-taisha|WD=Q714828|lt=Fushimi Inari Taisha}}, received imperial recognition.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://inari.jp/about/history/num04/ |title=稲荷勧請 |publisher=伏見稲荷大社 |accessdate=2024-01-21}}</ref>
== Shrines Awarded Shōichii == * {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrine}} – 807 AD * {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Shrine}} – 850 AD * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Shrine}} * {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha}}

'''Shōichii''' ({{ill|Senior First Rank|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}) is one of the highest [[Court Ranks in Japan|Ranks]] in Japan's system of {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō}}-based hierarchy and [[Shinkai (divine rank)|Shinkai (divine Ranks)]]. It Ranks above {{ill|Junior First Rank|ja|従一位|1=Junior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview == The Rank of Shōichii has been historically conferred upon individuals who made extraordinary contributions to the state, such as {{ill|Kampaku|ja|関白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kampaku|1=kampaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1199966}}, {{ill|Daijō-daijin|en|Daijō-daijin|WD=Q1157586|lt=Daijō Daijin}}, or {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}}. However, only seven individuals have ever received this Rank during their lifetime.<ref group="Note">These include {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|en|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|WD=Q1890878|lt=Fujiwara no Muchimaro}}, {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|en|Tachibana no Moroe|WD=Q3135863|lt=Tachibana no Moroe}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagate|en|Fujiwara no Nagate|WD=Q11623368|lt=Fujiwara no Nagate}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Katako|ja|源方子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Katako|1=Minamoto no Masako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564108}}, and {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|en|Sanjō Sanetomi|WD=Q516646|lt=Sanetomi Sanjō}}. Two of these individuals, Fujiwara no Muchimaro and Sanetomi Sanjō, passed away on the same day they received the Rank.</ref> Most instances of this Rank were posthumously awarded as {{ill|Zoi|lt=Zoi|lt=Zōi|ja|贈位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōi|qq=}}.
After {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, the Rank was formalized under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|lt=Shokuinryō|lt=Shokuinrei|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinrei|qq=}} of 1869.<ref>{{Egov law|215IO0000000325|位階令}}</ref> However, the Rank became symbolic as the role's corresponding duties ceased to exist.
For {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|en|Shinkai (divine rank)|WD=Q11591025|lt=Shin’i}}, many shrines achieved the Rank of Shōichii over time, particularly with the {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrines}}, which adopted this Rank after the main shrine, {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|en|Fushimi Inari-taisha|WD=Q714828|lt=Fushimi Inari Taisha}}, received imperial recognition.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://inari.jp/about/history/num04/ |title=稲荷勧請 |publisher=伏見稲荷大社 |accessdate=2024-01-21}}</ref>
== Shrines Awarded Shōichii == * {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrine}} – 807 AD * {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Shrine}} – 850 AD * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Shrine}} * {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha}}

Notes |- | {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|WD=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} || {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} 4 (720 AD) || Minister of the Right |- | '''{{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}}''' || {{ill|Jinki (era)|en|Jinki (era)|WD=Q1373210|lt=Shinkō}} 1 (724 AD) || Consort of Emperor Monmu |- | {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagate|en|Fujiwara no Nagate|WD=Q11623368|lt=Fujiwara no Nagate}} || {{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|WD=Q18520676|lt=Hōki}} 1 (770 AD) || Minister of the Left |- | {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga}} || {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō}} 6 (1917 AD) || Daimyō, posthumous award |}

== Overview == The Rank of Shōichii has been historically conferred upon individuals who made extraordinary contributions to the state, such as {{ill|Kampaku|ja|関白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kampaku|1=kampaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1199966}}, {{ill|Daijō-daijin|en|Daijō-daijin|WD=Q1157586|lt=Daijō Daijin}}, or {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}}. However, only seven individuals have ever received this Rank during their lifetime.<ref group="Note">These include {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|en|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|WD=Q1890878|lt=Fujiwara no Muchimaro}}, {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|en|Tachibana no Moroe|WD=Q3135863|lt=Tachibana no Moroe}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagate|en|Fujiwara no Nagate|WD=Q11623368|lt=Fujiwara no Nagate}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Katako|ja|源方子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Katako|1=Minamoto no Masako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564108}}, and {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|en|Sanjō Sanetomi|WD=Q516646|lt=Sanetomi Sanjō}}. Two of these individuals, Fujiwara no Muchimaro and Sanetomi Sanjō, passed away on the same day they received the Rank.</ref> Most instances of this Rank were posthumously awarded as {{ill|Zoi|lt=Zoi|lt=Zōi|ja|贈位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōi|qq=}}.
After {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, the Rank was formalized under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|lt=Shokuinryō|lt=Shokuinrei|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinrei|qq=}} of 1869.<ref>{{Egov law|215IO0000000325|位階令}}</ref> However, the Rank became symbolic as the role's corresponding duties ceased to exist.
For {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|en|Shinkai (divine rank)|WD=Q11591025|lt=Shin’i}}, many shrines achieved the Rank of Shōichii over time, particularly with the {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrines}}, which adopted this Rank after the main shrine, {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|en|Fushimi Inari-taisha|WD=Q714828|lt=Fushimi Inari Taisha}}, received imperial recognition.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://inari.jp/about/history/num04/ |title=稲荷勧請 |publisher=伏見稲荷大社 |accessdate=2024-01-21}}</ref>
== Shrines Awarded Shōichii == * {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrine}} – 807 AD * {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Shrine}} – 850 AD * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Shrine}} * {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha}}

'''Shōichii''' ({{ill|Senior First Rank|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}) is one of the highest [[Court Ranks in Japan|Ranks]] in Japan's system of {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō|1=Ritsuryō}}-based hierarchy and [[Shinkai (divine rank)|Shinkai (divine Ranks)]]. It Ranks above {{ill|Junior First Rank|ja|従一位|1=Junior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview == The Rank of Shōichii has been historically conferred upon individuals who made extraordinary contributions to the state, such as {{ill|Kampaku|ja|関白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kampaku|1=kampaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1199966}}, {{ill|Daijō-daijin|en|Daijō-daijin|WD=Q1157586|lt=Daijō Daijin|1=Daijō-daijin}}, or {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}}. However, only seven individuals have ever received this Rank during their lifetime.<ref group="Note">These include {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|en|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|WD=Q1890878|lt=Fujiwara no Muchimaro|1=Fujiwara no Muchimaro}}, {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|en|Tachibana no Moroe|WD=Q3135863|lt=Tachibana no Moroe|1=Tachibana no Moroe}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagate|en|Fujiwara no Nagate|WD=Q11623368|lt=Fujiwara no Nagate|1=Fujiwara no Nagate}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Katako|ja|源方子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Katako|1=Minamoto no Masako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564108}}, and {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|en|Sanjō Sanetomi|WD=Q516646|lt=Sanetomi Sanjō|1=Sanjō Sanetomi}}. Two of these individuals, Fujiwara no Muchimaro and Sanetomi Sanjō, passed away on the same day they received the Rank.</ref> Most instances of this Rank were posthumously awarded as {{ill|Zoi|lt=Zoi|lt=Zōi|ja|贈位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōi|qq=}}.
After {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the Rank was formalized under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|lt=Shokuinryō|lt=Shokuinrei|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinrei|qq=}} of 1869.<ref>{{Egov law|215IO0000000325|位階令}}</ref> However, the Rank became symbolic as the role's corresponding duties ceased to exist.
For {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|en|Shinkai (divine rank)|WD=Q11591025|lt=Shin’i|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}, many shrines achieved the Rank of Shōichii over time, particularly with the {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrines|1=Inari shrine}}, which adopted this Rank after the main shrine, {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|en|Fushimi Inari-taisha|WD=Q714828|lt=Fushimi Inari Taisha|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha}}, received imperial recognition.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://inari.jp/about/history/num04/ |title=稲荷勧請 |publisher=伏見稲荷大社 |accessdate=2024-01-21}}</ref>
== Shrines Awarded Shōichii == * {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo shrines}} – 807 AD * {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Shrine|1=Katori Jingū}} – 850 AD * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Shrine|1=Kashima Jingū}} * {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}}

It Ranks above {{ill|Junior First Rank|ja|従一位|1=Junior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview == The Rank of Shōichii has been historically conferred upon individuals who made extraordinary contributions to the state, such as {{ill|Kampaku|ja|関白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kampaku|1=kampaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1199966}}, {{ill|Daijō-daijin|en|Daijō-daijin|WD=Q1157586|lt=Daijō Daijin|1=Daijō-daijin}}, or {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}}. However, only seven individuals have ever received this Rank during their lifetime.<ref group="Note">These include {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|en|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|WD=Q1890878|lt=Fujiwara no Muchimaro|1=Fujiwara no Muchimaro}}, {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|en|Tachibana no Moroe|WD=Q3135863|lt=Tachibana no Moroe|1=Tachibana no Moroe}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagate|en|Fujiwara no Nagate|WD=Q11623368|lt=Fujiwara no Nagate|1=Fujiwara no Nagate}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Katako|ja|源方子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Katako|1=Minamoto no Masako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564108}}, and {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|en|Sanjō Sanetomi|WD=Q516646|lt=Sanetomi Sanjō|1=Sanjō Sanetomi}}. Two of these individuals, Fujiwara no Muchimaro and Sanetomi Sanjō, passed away on the same day they received the Rank.</ref> Most instances of this Rank were posthumously awarded as {{ill|Zoi|lt=Zoi|lt=Zōi|ja|贈位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōi|qq=}}.
After {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the Rank was formalized under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|lt=Shokuinryō|lt=Shokuinrei|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinrei|qq=}} of 1869.<ref>{{Egov law|215IO0000000325|位階令}}</ref> However, the Rank became symbolic as the role's corresponding duties ceased to exist.
For {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|en|Shinkai (divine rank)|WD=Q11591025|lt=Shin’i|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}, many shrines achieved the Rank of Shōichii over time, particularly with the {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrines|1=Inari shrine}}, which adopted this Rank after the main shrine, {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|en|Fushimi Inari-taisha|WD=Q714828|lt=Fushimi Inari Taisha|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha}}, received imperial recognition.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://inari.jp/about/history/num04/ |title=稲荷勧請 |publisher=伏見稲荷大社 |accessdate=2024-01-21}}</ref>
== Shrines Awarded Shōichii == * {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo shrines}} – 807 AD * {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Shrine|1=Katori Jingū}} – 850 AD * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Shrine|1=Kashima Jingū}} * {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}}
== Notable Individuals Awarded Shōichii == '''Bold''' indicates those awarded during their lifetime.
{| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Date of Conferment !! Notes |- | {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|WD=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito|1=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} || {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} 4 (720 AD) || Minister of the Right |- | '''{{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}}''' || {{ill|Jinki (era)|en|Jinki (era)|WD=Q1373210|lt=Shinkō|1=Jinki (era)}} 1 (724 AD) || Consort of Emperor Monmu |- | {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagate|en|Fujiwara no Nagate|WD=Q11623368|lt=Fujiwara no Nagate|1=Fujiwara no Nagate}} || {{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|WD=Q18520676|lt=Hōki|1=Hōki}} 1 (770 AD) || Minister of the Left |- | {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga|1=Oda Nobunaga}} || {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} 6 (1917 AD) || Daimyō, posthumous award |}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:شوئیچی]][[ja:正一位]][[ko:정1위]][[zh:正一位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:شوئیچی]][[ja:正一位]][[ko:정1위]][[zh:正一位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[fa:شوئیچی]][[ja:正一位]][[ko:정1위]][[zh:正一位]]
'''Shōichii''' ({{ill|Senior First Rank|ja|正一位|1=Senior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior First Rank|qq=}}) is one of the highest [[Court Ranks in Japan|Ranks]] in Japan's system of {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō|1=Ritsuryō}}-based hierarchy and [[Shinkai (divine rank)|Shinkai (divine Ranks)]]. It Ranks above {{ill|Junior First Rank|ja|従一位|1=Junior First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior First Rank|qq=}}.<ref>https://www.wakapoetry.net/background/society/court-offices-titles-and-Ranks/court-Ranks/</ref>
== Overview == The Rank of Shōichii has been historically conferred upon individuals who made extraordinary contributions to the state, such as {{ill|Kampaku|ja|関白|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kampaku|1=kampaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1199966}}, {{ill|Daijō-daijin|en|Daijō-daijin|WD=Q1157586|lt=Daijō Daijin|1=Daijō-daijin}}, or {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}}. However, only seven individuals have ever received this Rank during their lifetime.<ref group="Note">These include {{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|en|Fujiwara no Muchimaro|WD=Q1890878|lt=Fujiwara no Muchimaro|1=Fujiwara no Muchimaro}}, {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|en|Tachibana no Moroe|WD=Q3135863|lt=Tachibana no Moroe|1=Tachibana no Moroe}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagate|en|Fujiwara no Nagate|WD=Q11623368|lt=Fujiwara no Nagate|1=Fujiwara no Nagate}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Katako|ja|源方子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Katako|1=Minamoto no Masako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564108}}, and {{ill|Sanjō Sanetomi|en|Sanjō Sanetomi|WD=Q516646|lt=Sanetomi Sanjō|1=Sanjō Sanetomi}}. Two of these individuals, Fujiwara no Muchimaro and Sanetomi Sanjō, passed away on the same day they received the Rank.</ref> Most instances of this Rank were posthumously awarded as {{ill|Zoi|lt=Zoi|lt=Zōi|ja|贈位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōi|qq=}}.
After {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the Rank was formalized under the {{ill|Shokuinryō|lt=Shokuinryō|lt=Shokuinrei|ja|職員令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shokuinrei|qq=}} of 1869.<ref>{{Egov law|215IO0000000325|位階令}}</ref> However, the Rank became symbolic as the role's corresponding duties ceased to exist.
For {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|en|Shinkai (divine rank)|WD=Q11591025|lt=Shin’i|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}, many shrines achieved the Rank of Shōichii over time, particularly with the {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrines|1=Inari shrine}}, which adopted this Rank after the main shrine, {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|en|Fushimi Inari-taisha|WD=Q714828|lt=Fushimi Inari Taisha|1=Fushimi Inari-taisha}}, received imperial recognition.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://inari.jp/about/history/num04/ |title=稲荷勧請 |publisher=伏見稲荷大社 |accessdate=2024-01-21}}</ref>
== Shrines Awarded Shōichii == * {{ill|Kamo shrines|en|Kamo shrines|WD=Q1306606|lt=Kamo Shrine|1=Kamo shrines}} – 807 AD * {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Shrine|1=Katori Jingū}} – 850 AD * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Shrine|1=Kashima Jingū}} * {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}}
== Notable Individuals Awarded Shōichii == '''Bold''' indicates those awarded during their lifetime.
{| class="wikitable" ! Name !! Date of Conferment !! Notes |- | {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuhito|en|Fujiwara no Fuhito|WD=Q177517|lt=Fujiwara no Fuhito|1=Fujiwara no Fuhito}} || {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} 4 (720 AD) || Minister of the Right |- | '''{{ill|Fujiwara no Miyako|ja|藤原宮子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Miyako|1=Fujiwara no Miyako|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623185}}''' || {{ill|Jinki (era)|en|Jinki (era)|WD=Q1373210|lt=Shinkō|1=Jinki (era)}} 1 (724 AD) || Consort of Emperor Monmu |- | {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagate|en|Fujiwara no Nagate|WD=Q11623368|lt=Fujiwara no Nagate|1=Fujiwara no Nagate}} || {{ill|Hōki|en|Hōki|WD=Q18520676|lt=Hōki|1=Hōki}} 1 (770 AD) || Minister of the Left |- | {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga|1=Oda Nobunaga}} || {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} 6 (1917 AD) || Daimyō, posthumous award |}
